question_text: How does an object become positively charged through friction?

options.label: B
options.text: It loses electrons to another material

correct_answer: It loses electrons to another material
explanation: When two materials rub together during friction, electrons can be transferred from one material to the other. An object becomes positively charged when it loses electrons because it then has more protons (positive charges) than electrons (negative charges). The positive charges are no longer balanced by negative charges.
